title: rottlera paniculata
intro: Rottlera paniculata, also known as Emetic Nut, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and India, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an emetic and purgative, due to its ability to induce vomiting and relieve digestive issues. The health benefits of Rottlera paniculata include its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to relieve fever, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. Rottlera paniculata is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, and it can also be found in herbal supplements and traditional remedies.
